title: rubus chaerophyllus
intro: Rubus chaerophyllus, also known as the Woodland Bramble or Woodland Blackberry, is a species of wild blackberry native to Europe. It is a deciduous shrub with edible fruits and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The fruits and leaves of the Rubus chaerophyllus plant have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, colds, and fever, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its fruits can be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves.
